claim_gate.reason: Known polylog sparse-Fourier runtimes rely on structured sample schedules. Constant-arity attempts to recover those schedules from iid labels fail, while a genuinely new iid estimator remains open.

closure_certificates.interpretation: Polynomial iid labels cannot supply even one of the prescribed correlated offsets through this constant-arity signed-combination template at inverse-polynomial probability.
The finite-size union bound is inconclusive; it is not positive evidence for a decoder.

dcp_access_contract.available_postprocessing: polynomial-time classical processing of public labels and outcomes
dcp_access_contract.labels: independent uniform k in Z_(2^n), supplied rather than chosen
dcp_access_contract.observations: one stochastic X/Y outcome per phase register
dcp_access_contract.unavailable: evaluator, repeated labels, chosen shifts, filter queries, coherent score oracle

falsifiers_triggered: Existing significant-Fourier and HashToBins algorithms construct chosen, shifted, or correlated sample schedules absent from DCP.
Marginal randomness of a sparse-FFT query schedule does not make its joint distribution iid.
Constant-arity signed closure of polynomially many iid labels does not cover polynomially many prescribed offsets asymptotically.
Generic compressed sensing and full periodograms preserve random access but hide N-dimensional time or memory.
The restricted closure bound is not a general lower bound against a new random-example estimator.

mechanism_transfers.obstruction_or_open_bridge: DCP supplies iid labels after state measurement and cannot request the correlated query pattern.
mechanism_transfers.required_primitive: queries at x and x+h, subgroup restrictions, and repeated evaluation patterns
mechanism_transfers.source_locator: arXiv:1607.01842, random-access/query-access definitions and LPN limitation section
mechanism_transfers.supplied_input: an evaluator queried at adaptively or nonadaptively selected correlated group elements
mechanism_transfers.time_class_in_n: poly(n) under the stronger query oracle

mechanism_transfers.obstruction_or_open_bridge: Marginally random locations are insufficient: the locator constructs shared hash functions, shifts, filters, and correlated pairs. An iid-example estimator for the same bins is not supplied.
mechanism_transfers.required_primitive: filtered HashToBins measurements at shifted locations and randomly correlated sample pairs
mechanism_transfers.supplied_input: time-domain signal values at locations selected by randomized hash functions and filters
mechanism_transfers.time_class_in_n: poly(n) for one-sparse spectra with legal structured samples

mechanism_transfers.memory_class_in_n: generic formulations materialize N candidate coordinates
mechanism_transfers.obstruction_or_open_bridge: Random measurement compatibility resolves sample access only. The repository has no certified implicit solver whose arithmetic and storage are polynomial in n=log2(N).
mechanism_transfers.required_primitive: solve a one-sparse inverse problem from an unstructured random design
mechanism_transfers.source_locator: comparison class; no direct-transfer theorem claimed
mechanism_transfers.supplied_input: iid random rows of a partial Fourier design with noisy bounded responses
mechanism_transfers.time_class_in_n: generic solvers are poly(N), not certified poly(n)

mechanism_transfers.memory_class_in_n: Theta(N)=2^Theta(n) for a one-pass score table
mechanism_transfers.obstruction_or_open_bridge: It proves information recovery but loses to generic subexponential DCP sieves.
mechanism_transfers.required_primitive: score all N frequencies or run a length-N transform

mechanism_transfers.obstruction_or_open_bridge: The number of constant-arity expressions is polynomial and each nondegenerate label is uniform; prescribed offset coverage remains exponentially small. Products also compound measurement noise.
mechanism_transfers.required_primitive: post-hoc products or signed combinations intended to synthesize prescribed offsets
mechanism_transfers.source_locator: exact union-bound certificates in this artifact
mechanism_transfers.supplied_input: m=poly(n) iid public labels and local measurement outcomes

mechanism_transfers.obstruction_or_open_bridge: A valid construction must estimate useful hash/alias statistics from iid records, prove variance and worst-frequency bounds, and remain robust under f=1 bad registers.
mechanism_transfers.required_primitive: implicit heavy-frequency localization without chosen samples or N-sized candidate state
mechanism_transfers.source_locator: new adaptation obligation; not present in cited algorithms
mechanism_transfers.supplied_input: iid random labels with bounded stochastic complex-character observations

open_adaptation_contract.input: iid records (k,B,z) from the exact DCP measurement channel
open_adaptation_contract.kill_test: reject if any data structure, loop, oracle, preprocessing table, or advice has size N^Omega(1)
open_adaptation_contract.output: the complete d in Z_(2^n) with bounded error
open_adaptation_contract.required_novelty: estimate and decode multiscale hash statistics from iid records without reconstructing missing query pairs
open_adaptation_contract.resources: poly(n) states, time, memory, and bit precision
open_adaptation_contract.robustness: retain bounded error with hidden computational-basis bad registers at rate at most 1/n

summary: Audited 6 Fourier-localization mechanisms and 15 constant-arity closure certificates; 9/9 tail certificates rule out the tested schedule-synthesis template, with zero polylog iid decoders and zero general lower bounds proved.
